title: Prismatic lithium ion cell with positive polarity rigid container
abstract: Systems and methods are disclosed for battery cells with positive polarity rigid containers. In accordance with disclosed embodiments, the cell may include a container and a lid piece that couple together to form a rectangular prismatic geometry. An electrode assembly having positive and negative coils may be disposed within the cell, and the positive coil may be conductively coupled to the cell. In this way, the cell (e.g., both the lid and the container) may be positively polarized. Further, the electrode assembly may incorporate a jelly-roll or a stacked structure. In one embodiment, the lid piece may include a vent that opens in response to pressure in the cell surpassing an established threshold. The lid may further include a positive terminal, negative terminal, and a method for filling the cell with electrolyte. Another embodiment may provide a battery module include multiple cells with positive polarity rigid containers.
claims: 1. A method of manufacturing a battery cell, the method including: forming an electrode assembly having an electrode assembly body, a negative coil, and a positive coil, the electrode assembly forming a jelly-roll structure, the jelly-roll structure including a plurality of layers, the plurality of layers including a first insulation layer, a second insulation layer, a negative coil layer, and a positive coil layer, the first insulation layer having a first insulation layer edge and a second insulation layer edge opposite the first insulation layer edge, the second insulation layer having a third insulation layer edge and a fourth insulation layer edge opposite the third insulation layer edge; placing the negative coil layer between and in direct physical contact with each one of the first and second insulation layers, the negative coil layer having a first negative coil layer edge and a second negative coil layer edge opposite the first negative coil layer edge; aligning the second negative coil layer edge with the second insulation layer edge of the first insulation layer and with the fourth insulation layer edge of the second insulation layer; disposing the positive coil layer under and in direct physical contact with the second insulation layer, the positive coil layer having a first positive coil layer edge and a second positive coil layer edge opposite the first positive coil layer edge; aligning the first positive coil layer edge with the first insulation layer edge of the first insulation layer and with the third insulation layer edge of the second insulation layer, the first and second insulation layers being centered with respect to the jelly-roll structure, the negative coil layer extending in a first direction beyond the first and second insulation layers, the positive coil layer extending in a second direction opposite the first direction beyond the first and second insulation layers; rolling the plurality of layers to form the jelly-roll structure; placing the electrode assembly in a container; and coupling a lid to the container.
